raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year> I paid {$3500.00} to XXXX XXXX for the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. The merchant 's no-refund contract was presented 6 minutes after payment was collected, confirmed by timestamped evidence. I filed a CFPB complaint on XX/XX/year> ( complaint number XXXX ). Since then, Citibank has issued two additional letters : on XX/XX/year> closing the dispute because the merchant was unwilling to issue credit, and on XX/XX/year> requesting proof the merchant agreed to credit my account. Both letters reflect a fundamental misapplication of the FCBA. A chargeback does not require merchant cooperation Citibank has the authority to forcibly reverse the charge independent of the merchant 's position. Citibank has repeatedly failed to review timestamped evidence on its merits and continues to move the goalposts rather than exercise its chargeback authority.
